meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Overcoming Design Gaps with AI Workflows
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with UI. Learn a high-speed freelancer workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade SaaS products without design skills.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no-design' barrier by leveraging component-driven AI workflows. Use Antigravity to generate clean, semantic UI structures and Claude Code to iterate on layout logic. By treating design as a code-first system rather than an artistic endeavor, you can ship production-ready, aesthetically consistent SaaS applications at lightning speed.
content.intro:
The blank canvas is a freelancer's worst nightmare. You have the backend logic, the database schema, and the API integrations dialed in, but the interface remains a jagged mess of unaligned divs and default browser styling. Clients don't pay for clean code; they pay for the experience, and your lack of design intuition is hitting your bottom line.

It’s time to stop fighting the pixels. By shifting from a 'designer' mindset to a 'system architect' mindset, you can automate your UI output. Using Antigravity for structural precision and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you will transform your development pipeline into a high-velocity engine that produces professional interfaces every single time.

steps.title: Establish the Design System Constraints
steps.description: Define your color palette, spacing scale, and typography before writing a single line of code. This prevents the 'Frankenstein UI' effect.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Tailwind CSS configuration file that defines a professional, modern SaaS color palette (blues/grays), a modular 4px spacing scale, and a sans-serif typography system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file tailwind.config.js --spec 'modern saas design system'

steps.title: Architect the Layout Skeleton
steps.description: Build the structural shell using semantic HTML5. Focus on responsiveness and layout flow rather than aesthetic flair.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout component with a sidebar, top navigation bar, and main content area using semantic HTML5 and Tailwind CSS classes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/components/Layout.tsx --instruction 'implement responsive flexbox sidebar and header'

steps.title: Componentize the Interface
steps.description: Break the UI into atomic components. This modular approach ensures consistency across client projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable UI library of high-conversion components: button variants, input fields with error states, and data tables with sorting icons.

steps.title: Automate Visual Refinement
steps.description: Use Claude Code to inject 'polish'—shadows, rounded corners, and hover states—into your existing components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply professional-grade UI polish: add soft shadows (shadow-sm), subtle border radii, and smooth hover transitions to all existing button and card components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --glob 'src/components/**/*.tsx' --instruction 'add professional shadow, rounded-lg, and transition-all to all interactive elements'

steps.title: Stress Test for Responsiveness
steps.description: Force the UI to break at various breakpoints to ensure your client's mobile experience is as robust as the desktop view.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current layout for mobile responsiveness issues. Suggest specific Tailwind class adjustments for screens smaller than 768px.

pro_tips: Always force a 'dark mode' variant early; it hides poor color choices better than light mode.
Use 'Design Tokens' as comments in your CSS to ensure your future self or other devs maintain consistency.
Limit your color palette to three primary colors plus shades of slate; professional design is about restraint, not variety.
Leverage Claude Code's ability to read your entire directory to ensure component props are consistent across the app.
If a UI element takes more than 15 minutes to code, you are over-engineering; use a pre-built component library instead.
content.next_steps: Once your UI workflow is stabilized, begin building a private 'component vault' repository so you can clone your own high-quality boilerplate for every new client project.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design limitations bottleneck your freelance revenue. Master the tools, refine your system, and start shipping high-end SaaS projects in record time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
